channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: BJP acknowledges youth anger over NEET mismanagement and protest crackdown concerns
item.description: The article discusses BJP workers' concerns about the government's handling of protests, particularly the crackdown on student demonstrations related to the NEET exam mismanagement. Workers admit the ...

item.title: Prison Officials Monitor Lawyer-Client Communications in Constitutional Case
item.description: The article details the ongoing constitutional case involving Tundu Lissu, where his lawyers allege that prison officials at Ukonga Prison are systematically monitoring their communications with him. ...

item.title: Pulse Asia Survey Shows Sara Duterte as Top Choice for 2028 Presidential Election
item.description: A recent Pulse Asia survey conducted from June 28 to July 3 reveals that Vice President Sara Duterte is the preferred presidential candidate for the May 2028 elections, with 49% of respondents choosin...

item.title: Winnipeg NDP Retains The Pas-Kameesak MLA Seat in By-Election
item.description: The Winnipeg NDP has successfully retained the The Pas-Kameesak MLA seat in a by-election, maintaining their hold over the northern Manitoba constituency. This outcome underscores the party's continue...

item.title: AI chatbots pose risks as mental health professionals caution against replacing human therapists
item.description: The article discusses growing concerns among mental health professionals about the increasing use of AI chatbots like ChatGPT for emotional support and mental health advice. While these tools offer co...

item.title: Fatboy Slim Collaborates with Simon Pegg and Brighton Gay Men’s Chorus at Big Beach Boutique 7
item.description: Fatboy Slim, real name Norman Cook, delivered a special performance at the Big Beach Boutique 7 festival in Brighton, collaborating with comedian Simon Pegg and the Brighton Gay Men’s Chorus. The even...

item.title: Red Tape as a Hidden Tax: Challenges Faced by Small Businesses in South Africa
item.description: The article highlights how bureaucratic red tape is a significant burden on small businesses in South Africa, acting as a hidden tax that stifles growth and job creation. Entrepreneurs face delays, co...

item.title: President Marcos Commits to Holding First BARMM Elections in September 2026
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has vowed to proceed with the long-delayed first Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) parliamentary elections on September 14, 2026, despite legal hur...

item.title: Alberta liquor producers reconsider U.S. expansion plans as proposed American tariffs threaten exports
item.description: Alberta liquor producers and retailers are preparing for potential disruptions after the United States announced plans to impose 50 per cent tariffs on Canadian alcohol products beginning Aug. 19. The...

item.title: Kenya Crackdown: Arrest of Drug Mule and 16 Pellets Seized in Mombasa Operation
item.description: Anti-narcotics officers arrested 67-year-old Hilton Chopeta at Moi International Airport after intercepting him with 44 cocaine pellets worth KSh 1.5 million hidden in his stomach. The suspect, headin...

item.title: France to ban social media use by minors in landmark EU legislation
item.description: French lawmakers are poised to implement a groundbreaking policy that would prohibit children under the age of 15 from accessing social media platforms, marking the first such measure in Europe. The p...

item.title: Pakistan and Iran Commit to Enhanced Cooperation in Key Areas
item.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi emphasized Pakistan's readiness to deepen cooperation with Iran across all sectors during a meeting with Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni. The discussions, held...

item.title: Double Standards in Labeling State Actions as Terrorism
item.description: The article critiques the double standard in labeling actions as terrorism versus 'strikes' when committed by state actors versus non-state entities. It argues that Western media and political institu...

item.title: Brad Pitt's youngest daughter Vivienne files to change her legal name
item.description: Vivienne Jolie-Pitt, 18, has filed a petition in Los Angeles County Superior Court to legally change her name from Vivienne Marcheline Jolie-Pitt to Vivienne Marcheline Jolie. This follows the lead of...

item.title: Frequent TV Viewing in Midlife Linked to Brain Structural Changes
item.description: A study published in *Alzheimer's* journal reveals that excessive television consumption during midlife is associated with measurable changes in brain structure. Researchers analyzed data from 1,700 p...

item.title: UK's Post-Brexit Strategy in Africa: Emphasizing Mutual Benefits and Competing with Global Powers
item.description: The article discusses the United Kingdom's efforts to strengthen its ties with African nations post-Brexit, focusing on creating mutually beneficial relationships. Ben Ainsley, the acting trade commis...

item.title: Reps Committee Summons NSA and Foreign Affairs Minister Over Investigation Into Alleged Illegal Investment Council
item.description: The House of Representatives Ad Hoc Committee investigating the activities of the Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC) has summoned the National Security Adviser (NSA), Nuhu Ribad...

item.title: Provinces Allow Direct-to-Consumer Alcohol Sales Across Canada
item.description: Eight Canadian provinces have agreed to permit residents to purchase alcohol directly from producers in other provinces, marking a significant shift in interprovincial trade policies. New Brunswick wa...

item.title: Arizona GOP primary results: Andy Biggs wins gubernatorial race with strong margin
item.description: Rep. Andy Biggs (R-AZ) secured a decisive victory in the Republican gubernatorial primary, winning with 71% of the vote according to NBC News. The win comes despite a Washington Post report revealing ...

item.title: President Ruto Meets Content Creators to Discuss Youth Issues
item.description: Content creators CassyPool and KRG The Don visited State House to meet President William Ruto, discussing youth concerns. The meeting sparked mixed online reactions, with some praising the duo for eng...
